description_long: This object is a tall, upright stone stele with a rounded top, featuring intricate ancient Egyptian iconography and hieroglyphic inscriptions on its front face. The front is divided into several horizontal registers, each filled with detailed depictions of deities, figures, and symbolic motifs, all rendered in a traditional Egyptian style. The uppermost section displays a winged sun disk and a large circular motif, possibly representing the sun god Ra. The sides are thin and unadorned, while the back is plain and lacks any visible decoration or inscription. The material appears to be a type of stone, possibly limestone or sandstone, with a painted or inlaid surface to highlight the carvings. The overall appearance suggests it is a commemorative or funerary monument, typical of those found in ancient Egyptian urban or temple settings.
description: A tall, rounded stone stele richly decorated with ancient Egyptian carvings and hieroglyphs on its front, with plain sides and back.
description_view_0: The front view shows detailed Egyptian iconography and hieroglyphic inscriptions, divided into horizontal registers.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals the thin profile of the stele, with no visible decoration.
description_view_2: The back view is plain, showing a smooth, undecorated stone surface.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, displaying the thin, undecorated edge of the stele.
attribute_inscription: The front face is covered with ancient Egyptian hieroglyphic inscriptions, arranged in horizontal bands, with high legibility and traditional style.
attribute_ornamentation: The front is richly ornamented with carved and painted Egyptian motifs, including deities, figures, and a winged sun disk at the top.
attribute_base_design: The base is flat and rectangular, with no visible steps or additional decorative features, blending seamlessly with the rest of the stone.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is rounded and smooth, with no additional decoration or unique features beyond the continuation of the front's ornamentation.
